search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae routine pediatric follow-up child health clinical guideline Dubai
site:doh.gov.ae well-child visit standard Abu Dhabi
site:ehs.gov.ae child health vaccinations periodic assessment growth development UAE
site:aap.org Bright Futures well child visit guideline
candidate_sources_rejected: EHS Child Health and Vaccinations service page
rejection_reasons: The EHS page is an operational service description rather than an exact clinical standard.
The AAP materials were not selected over the current UAE DoH standard and were not required to support the mapped UAE well-child fields.
population_applicability: Partial: exact source covers children aged 1 month to 6 years, while the baseline routine pediatric workflow has no explicit age limit.
setting_applicability: Partial: exact source is a regulated Abu Dhabi well-child screening service standard; the baseline is setting-agnostic.
UAE_applicability: Verified for DoH-licensed well-child services in Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates; broader UAE application is not asserted.
recency_verification: Official revised V10 standard published December 2025 and opened on 2026-07-11; the document states effective date TBD and revision December 2029.
superseded_check: Current revised official copy identified on the DoH website on 2026-07-11.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Directly supports the well-child visit identity, parent history, physical examination, vital-sign, developmental, vaccination, and documentation context for the stated age range.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines the 1-month-to-6-year population and requires parent history, age-specific physical examination, vital signs, developmental milestones, screening, vaccination discussion, and EMR documentation.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Directly supports mapped growth, feeding, activity, sleep, immunization, weight, height, and age-linked follow-up documentation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Specifies age-linked weight, length or height, head circumference, growth plotting, feeding and medical history, developmental milestones, screening, immunization review, and caregiver counselling.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Directly supports mapped caregiver-report, counselling, and documented plan fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Requires effective record keeping and documentation of screening findings, caregiver education, consent, and refusal.

unresolved_source_gaps: The standard is limited to ages 1 month through 6 years and an Abu Dhabi screening setting.
The published copy states that its effective date is TBD.
Acute symptom progress, broad normal examination conclusions, fixed return precautions, and unrelated examination prompts remain unsupported.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
